# NoteForAI

> AI-native persistent memory backend with file-system-like API, Git versioning, full-text search, and MCP support.

NoteForAI gives AI assistants a persistent, structured memory store. Notes are organized in a per-token namespace (one namespace per AI agent/user), stored as plain Markdown files on disk with automatic Git versioning for every change. It exposes both an HTTP REST API and an MCP (Model Context Protocol) endpoint so AI tools can integrate with zero friction.

## Docs

- [README (English)](https://github.com/builder-yanzhang/NoteForAi/blob/master/README.md): Full setup guide, API reference, MCP config examples
- [README (ç®€ä˝“ä¸­ć–‡)](https://github.com/builder-yanzhang/NoteForAi/blob/master/README_zh-CN.md): Chinese Simplified
- [README (ć—Ąćś¬čŞž)](https://github.com/builder-yanzhang/NoteForAi/blob/master/README_ja.md): Japanese
- [API Reference](https://github.com/builder-yanzhang/NoteForAi/blob/master/README.md#api-reference): All HTTP endpoints
- [MCP Integration](https://github.com/builder-yanzhang/NoteForAi/blob/master/README.md#mcp-integration): MCP config for Claude Desktop, Cursor, and other clients

## Key Features

- **File-system-like API**: write, read, append, delete, list, tree â€” intuitive path-based note management
- **Git versioning**: every write is a commit; full history, diff, and revert support
- **Full-text search**: CJK-aware search powered by Bleve (no external DB required)
- **MCP Streamable HTTP**: native MCP protocol via `/{token}/mcp` â€” works with Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf, and any MCP client
- **MCP stdio**: also supports stdio transport for local integrations
- **Multi-tenant**: each API token gets an isolated namespace (separate directory + Git repo)
- **Self-hostable**: single Go binary, no database, minimal dependencies
- **Web dashboard**: built-in UI for note browsing, editing, version history, recycle bin
- **Hosted service**: available at `https://noteforai.com` â€” no self-hosting required

## HTTP API Endpoints

**Authentication**: the token is embedded in the URL path as `/{token}/<action>`. There is no `Authorization` header â€” the token *is* the path prefix. Example: `POST https://noteforai.com/nfa_abc123.../write`.

**Methods**: every endpoint accepts both `POST` (JSON body) and `GET` (query string) for parameters. POST + JSON is the canonical form. Read-style endpoints also accept a trailing path: `GET /{token}/read/path/to/note.md`.

| Endpoint | Params | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| `POST /create_token` | â€” | Create a new token (no auth required) |
| `POST /{token}/write` | `path`, `content` | Create or overwrite a note |
| `POST /{token}/read` | `path` | Read a note by path |
| `POST /{token}/append` | `path`, `content` | Append content to an existing note |
| `POST /{token}/delete` | `path` | Soft-delete a file **or directory**. For directories this is **recursive**: every file under the path is soft-deleted (each with its own restore entry). There is no `recursive` / `confirm` flag â€” passing a directory path triggers recursive deletion immediately. Undo via `deleted` + `revert` per file. |
| `POST /{token}/list` | `path` | List entries in a directory (non-recursive) |
| `POST /{token}/tree` | `path` | Recursive directory tree |
| `POST /{token}/search` | `query` (or `q`), `path` | CJK-aware full-text search, optionally scoped to a subpath |
| `POST /{token}/history` | `path`, `limit` | Git commit history for a note |
| `POST /{token}/diff` | `path`, `commit` | Unified diff between a note and a past commit |
| `POST /{token}/revert` | `path`, `commit` | Restore a note (or a soft-deleted entry) to a specific commit |
| `POST /{token}/deleted` | `limit` | List recoverable soft-deleted files (recycle bin) |
| `POST /{token}/destroy` | â€” | **Destroy the entire token namespace** (all notes, index, git history). This is a token-level operation, *not* a per-note delete â€” it takes no `path` parameter. The token directory is moved to a trash area and cleaned up after `TRASH_DAYS`. |
| `POST /{token}/mcp` | â€” | MCP Streamable HTTP endpoint (for MCP clients) |

## MCP Configuration

### Hosted (Streamable HTTP)

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "noteforai": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "https://noteforai.com/{your-token}/mcp"
    }
  }
}
```

### Self-hosted (stdio)

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "noteforai": {
      "command": "/path/to/noteforai",
      "args": ["--mcp", "--data", "/path/to/data"],
      "env": {
        "NFA_TOKEN": "your-token"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Usage Pattern for AI Agents

When starting a session, call `tree()` to orient yourself. Use path-based organization: `projects/myapp/notes.md`, `daily/2024-01-15.md`, `research/topic.md`. Append to existing notes rather than overwriting when adding incremental information.

## Tech Stack

- **Language**: Go 1.21+
- **Search**: Bleve (embedded full-text, CJK support)
- **Storage**: Filesystem + Git (via go-git)
- **Protocol**: HTTP REST + MCP (Streamable HTTP + stdio)
- **UI**: Embedded single-file HTML (go:embed)
- **Deployment**: Single binary, Docker, or Fly.io

## Self-Hosting

```bash
# Docker
docker run -d -p 8080:8080 -v ./data:/data noteforai/noteforai

# From source
git clone https://github.com/builder-yanzhang/NoteForAi
cd NoteForAi && go build -o noteforai .
./noteforai --port 8080 --data ./data
```
